title: Past Perfect
definition: The Past Perfect tense shows that one past action happened before another past action — the 'earlier past'.

structure.positive: I had finished my work before she arrived.
structure.negative: I had not (hadn't) finished my work before she arrived.
structure.question: Had you finished your work before she arrived?
structure.whQuestion: What had you finished before she arrived?

usage: Use Past Perfect to show which of two past actions happened first (The train had left before we reached the station), and to describe a past action completed before another past time reference (By 2015, he had already finished college).
examples: The train had already left when we reached the station.
She had finished her homework before dinner.
By the time I arrived, they had left.
I had never seen snow before I visited Shimla.
Had you eaten before the meeting started?
He had not finished his project when the deadline came.
We had just sat down when the phone rang.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: Had you finished the report before the meeting?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: Yes, I had finished it the night before.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: Was he there when you arrived?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: No, he had already left by then.

commonMistakes.why: Use the past participle (V3) 'gone' after 'had', not the past simple form 'went'.

commonMistakes.right: When I arrived, she had left.
commonMistakes.why: Use Past Perfect to show the leaving happened before the arriving.
Use the past participle 'eaten', not 'ate', after 'had'.

quiz.question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: The train had leave before we arrived.
The train had left before we arrived.
The train has left before we arrived.
The train left had before we arrived.
quiz.answer: The train had left before we arrived.
quiz.explanation: Formula: had + V3, for the earlier of two past actions.

quiz.question: By the time she called, I ___ (leave) already.
quiz.explanation: The leaving happened before the calling, so use Past Perfect.

quiz.question: Which shows the correct order using Past Perfect?
options: I had eaten before he arrived.
I ate before he had arrived.
I have eaten before he arrived.
quiz.answer: I had eaten before he arrived.
quiz.explanation: The earlier action (eating) uses Past Perfect; the later action uses Past Simple.

quiz.sentence: She had ate dinner before we came.
quiz.correction: She had eaten dinner before we came.
